lessons.title: Learn: Process and Service Management
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 07: Process and Service Management

Processes are running programs. Service management means understanding long-running background programs and controlling their lifecycle safely.

## Learning Objectives

- Inspect running processes with `ps`, `top`, and `pgrep`
- Start background jobs and track their PIDs
- Stop processes using `kill` and `pkill`
- Inspect listening services with `ss`
- Read system state quickly (`uptime`, `free`)

## Core Topics

### 1) Process Inspection

```bash
ps aux
ps -ef
pgrep -f nginx
```

### 2) Real-Time Monitoring

```bash
top -b -n 1 | head -n 10
```

### 3) Process Control

```bash
sleep 300 &
kill <pid>
pkill -f 'sleep 300'
```

### 4) Service and Port Visibility

```bash
ss -lnt
ss -lntp
```

### 5) Safety Rules

- Always identify process correctly before killing it
- Prefer graceful terminate first (`kill`) before force kill (`kill -9`)
- Avoid killing unknown root/system processes

After Learn, complete command practice, then exercises, then MCQ.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Practice process inspection and basic controls.

```bash
cd ~ && mkdir -p ~/lab7
ps aux | head -n 10
ps -ef | head -n 10
top -b -n 1 | head -n 8
pgrep -f bash
sleep 300 & echo PROCESS_STARTED
ps -ef | grep '[s]leep 300'
pgrep -f 'sleep 300' | head -n 1
kill $(pgrep -f 'sleep 300' | head -n 1) && echo PROCESS_STOPPED
pgrep -f 'sleep 300' || echo NOT_RUNNING
```

tasks.description: C01: Create lab7 directory in home.
C02: Show first lines of ps aux output.
C03: Show first lines of ps -ef output.
C04: Capture one snapshot using top batch mode.
C05: Search for bash process IDs.
C06: Start sleep process in background and confirm marker.
C07: Verify sleep 300 appears in process list.
C08: Print PID of sleep 300 process.
C09: Stop one sleep 300 process and confirm stop marker.
C10: Confirm sleep 300 is not running.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Practice service visibility, resource checks, and force-stop patterns.

```bash
uptime
free -m
ss -lnt
ss -lntp | head -n 12
ps -eo pid,comm,%cpu,%mem --sort=-%cpu | head -n 10
ps -eo pid,comm,%mem --sort=-%mem | head -n 10
sleep 400 & echo SECOND_PROCESS_STARTED
pkill -f 'sleep 400' && echo SECOND_PROCESS_STOPPED
pgrep -f 'sleep 400' || echo SECOND_NOT_RUNNING
ps -ef | grep '[b]ash' | head -n 5
```

tasks.description: C11: Show system uptime and load.
C12: Show memory summary in MB.
C13: List listening TCP ports.
C14: List listening TCP ports with process info preview.
C15: Show top CPU-consuming processes.
C16: Show top memory-consuming processes.
C17: Start another background sleep process.
C18: Stop sleep 400 using pkill.
C19: Confirm sleep 400 process is gone.
C20: Show bash-related processes with ps + grep.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Complete all exercises to prove process and service control skills.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Start sleep 500 in background and confirm with ps grep.
E2: Show PID list of sleep 500 using pgrep.
E3: Stop sleep 500 using pkill and print marker KILLED_500.
E4: Verify no sleep 500 remains running.
E5: Show listening ports and include only LISTEN lines using grep.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: Process and Service Management

Answer using option index (0-3). Example: submit 2.

tasks.description: Q1: Which command finds process IDs by name pattern?
Q2: Which command sends a signal to a process by PID?
Q3: Which command can show real-time process activity?
Q4: Which command lists listening TCP sockets?
Q5: Best safe practice before killing a process is to:
tasks.validation_rule: ["Use kill -9 always","Reboot system first","Confirm PID and command with ps","Delete /proc entry manually"]
